steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The claim's decisive phrase is that Newland “compares his intellect favorably” to other men. I need a quotation that directly shows his intellectual superiority through superior reading, thinking, or knowledge.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly shows this comparison: Newland felt “distinctly the superior” of these men because he had “read more, thought more, and even seen a good deal more of the world, than any other man.” The other three do not illustrate this intellectual comparison — B and C lack it entirely, and D focuses on social dynamics rather than thinking-based superiority.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
